column1: # Rust is the producer/oracle that fixes the expected values. Swift reads the same fixture as a consumer.
# Columns: id <TAB> expected <TAB> scalars(space-separated hex code points) <TAB> description
# Target is the policy for "display text fields" (no ASCII-only requirement; length limits are separate).

column4: Todhri Letter A (newly assigned in Unicode 16.0 -> accepted)
U+088F (Cn in 16.0; assigned in 17.0 -> rejected under the 16.0 baseline)
U+E000 (Private Use = Co). Evidence it is accepted because only Cn is rejected

column1: # Enumerate all 12 Bidi_Control code points as negatives (avoid transcription gaps in Swift).
# Also fix the accepted side of control boundaries (current spec rejects only C0=0000..001F and C1=0080..009F).

column4: U+007F DELETE (outside C0/C1 = accepted under the current spec; intentional)
U+00A0 NO-BREAK SPACE (just after C1; accepted; unchanged under NFC)
